title: Tuning instability in suspended monolayer 2D materials

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies how suspended monolayer graphene and MoS2 undergo controllable, reversible instability under in-plane shear using a push-to-shear micro-mechanical device inside SEM. The authors identify a primary wrinkling transition and a secondary wrinkle-splitting transition, quantify critical strains/stresses, and use a scaling law to estimate bending stiffness; MD simulations attribute the different loading/unloading paths to local stress redistribution.
measurement_types: in situ nanomechanical shear force-displacement measurement

figures.description: Schematic and example images of four fundamental mechanical experiments for 2D materials: tension, compression, bending, and shearing.
Schematic of shearing in 2D materials and its relevance to 2D-material-based systems.
Experimental setup of the push-to-shear micro-mechanical device used to shear suspended monolayer 2D materials.
In situ SEM images and corresponding 3D morphologies showing reversible smoothing-wrinkling-smoothing of monolayer graphene during loading and unloading.
Shear stress-strain curve during loading-unloading, with markers indicating onset/disappearance of instabilities.
In situ images and cartoons of primary wrinkling evolution in monolayer graphene as shear strain increases and then unloads.
Normalized wrinkle deflection profiles from AFM/3D morphology scans along two principal directions for graphene and MoS2, compared with sinusoidal fits.
Statistics of wrinkle orientation angles for graphene and MoS2.
Theoretical and experimental normalized wrinkling wavelength versus shear strain for graphene under different assumed bending stiffness values.
Theoretical and experimental normalized wrinkling wavelength versus shear strain for MoS2 under different assumed bending stiffness values.
Scatter plot of bending stiffness values extracted from six samples of graphene and MoS2.
In situ images and cartoons of secondary instability and recovery in monolayer graphene.
Zoomed 3D morphologies and cartoons showing loading-path wrinkle splitting into smaller wrinkles (wrinklon-like propagation).
Zoomed 3D morphologies and cartoons showing unloading-path smoothing without wrinkle merging.
Wavelength versus shear strain during first and second instabilities, comparing theory and experiment.
Shear stress-strain curve highlighting stress drops during secondary instability, with inset SEM images.
Schematic of the MD shear simulation setup for monolayer graphene.
MD snapshots showing nucleation, propagation, and completion of secondary wrinkle splitting during loading and smoothing during unloading.
MD average shear stress-strain and potential energy curves for loading and unloading.
MD half-wavelength versus shear strain showing hysteresis between loading and unloading paths.
MD minimum principal stress fields at different shear strains, showing stress redistribution and wrinklon formation.
MD deﬂection profiles and compressive stress distributions along selected cross-sections.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw in situ SEM videos/images of suspended graphene and MoS2 during push-to-shear loading-unloading, raw force-displacement data from the nanoindenter/actuator, Raman spectra for layer confirmation, and AFM morphology scans used to obtain wrinkle profiles. It also mentions digital image correlation for strain measurement during the in situ test.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper plots processed/derived quantities including shear stress-strain curves, normalized wrinkle profiles, wrinkle wavelength versus shear strain, wrinkle orientation statistics, and extracted bending stiffness values. Theoretical curves from continuum analysis and MD-derived stress/energy/wavelength plots are also shown.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The data supporting the findings are included in the main text and supplementary information files. Raw data can be obtained from the corresponding author upon request. Source data are also provided as a Source Data file with this paper.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only generally: in situ SEM video/images, force-displacement data from the SEM-incorporated Hysitron PI-85 nanoindenter, Raman spectra, AFM morphology measurements, and MD simulation outputs. The paper does not specify file formats or a public repository.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted shear stress, shear strain, wrinkle wavelength, wrinkle orientation angle, normalized wrinkle profiles, and fitted bending stiffness values. These are used to generate the main and supplementary figures.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. The paper explicitly states that source data are provided as a Source Data file with the paper, implying figure-level processed data are available for replotting.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned in the paper text. MD simulations were performed with LAMMPS and visualization with OVITO, but these are software tools, not provided scripts.
dataset_characterization.data_organization: Mixed / figure-based source data implied, but not explicitly organized in the text
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: Source data are explicitly provided, so figure-level replotting is likely possible for the published plots. However, raw measurement files are not publicly deposited and are only available on request, so full raw-data reanalysis is not directly enabled from the paper text alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper distinguishes between raw experimental measurements and derived quantities used in figures, but only the existence of a Source Data file is explicit. Supplementary movies and supplementary figures are mentioned, suggesting additional figure-linked data exist, but their exact contents are not specified in the text. The MD results are simulation outputs, not experimental raw data.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods and Data availability sections state that raw data can be obtained from the corresponding author upon request, and the experiments generate SEM images/videos, force-displacement data, Raman spectra, and AFM morphology data.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The Data availability section explicitly says that source data are provided as a Source Data file with this paper.
classification_prior.both_evidence: Both raw data and source data are implied by the data availability statement.
data_availability_section_relevant: Raw data can be obtained from the corresponding author upon request.
Source data are also provided as a Source Data file with this paper.
The data that supports the findings of the study are included in the main text and supplementary information files.
classification_prior.review_notes: The paper is not a repository-style data paper; it is a mechanics paper with figure-linked source data and request-only raw data. Distinguish carefully between raw SEM/AFM/force data and processed quantities such as wavelength, stress, strain, and fitted bending stiffness. The presence of supplementary movies suggests additional raw/visual data, but their exact file types are not specified.
